claims.title: PROFIT-1 standing: cost gates double-count trading costs (net-of-fee edge compared to a fee threshold wall) in both demo and live paths
claims.assertion: cost_gate_moderate_with_slippage and cost_gate_live_with_slippage require cell.shrunk_bps >= 2*(fee_rate+slippage)*1e4 / win_rate * 1.3, but shrunk_bps is derived from net_pnl_bps (gross minus entry+exit fees), so round-trip cost is deducted once in the realized metric and demanded again in the threshold — an effective ~2.6x–4.3x cost wall on an already-net edge (win_rate floor 0.3, safety multiplier 1.3).
claims.evidence: gates.rs:145 `fee_bps = 2.0*(fee_rate+slippage)*10_000.0`; gates.rs:147-151 and 475-478 `threshold_bps = fee_bps / wr * cost_gate_safety_multiplier` compared to cell.shrunk_bps. Producer chain: realized_edge_stats.py:405-430 `net_bps = _bps(gross_pnl_usd - entry_fee_usd - exit_fee_usd, ...)`; james_stein_estimator.py:384 `raw_values = [stats[k].mean_net_bps ...]` feeds shrunk_bps. Config: settings/risk_control_rules/risk_config*.toml `cost_gate_win_rate_floor=0.3`, `cost_gate_safety_multiplier=1.3`. Matches PROFIT-1 confirmed 2026-06-14 and operator-ratified-not-fixed 2026-07-09.
claims.impact: Suppressed-valid-edge annuity: cells with genuinely positive after-cost edge (e.g. net +5 bps against an ~11-13 bps round-trip taker cost) are rejected unless net edge exceeds ~28-47 bps. This over-gating is operator-ratified as a standing decision (hard-boundary priority), so it must not be silently changed, but it remains the largest single quant-side headwind to net-PnL discovery and should be re-decided explicitly, e.g. threshold on gross edge or a reduced multiplier on net edge.
claims.fix_hint: Bring to operator as an explicit re-decision (not a silent code change): either compare gross edge to the cost wall, or compare net edge to a residual-safety margin (e.g. fee_bps*(multiplier-1)); keep live fail-closed posture.

claims.title: DSR gate deviates from cited Bailey-LdP Eq.8: E[max SR_k] omits sqrt(Var(SR_k)) scaling, making DSR effectively always-block for K>=2 on per-trade Sharpe inputs
claims.assertion: _compute_expected_max_sharpe returns (1-γ)Φ⁻¹(1-1/K)+γΦ⁻¹(1-1/(Ke)) with implicit unit cross-trial Sharpe variance. Callers feed per-trade OOS Sharpe (observed_sharpe ← oos_sharpe, n_trials = n_eff) without trial_sharpes, so for K>=2 the deflation threshold (≈1.16σ at K=5, 1.539σ at K=10) sits far above any plausible per-trade Sharpe, driving DSR≈0 regardless of true edge. The repo's own walk-forward skill §2.2 formula includes the sqrt(Var(SR)) factor that the code drops.
claims.evidence: dsr_gate.py:227-255 (_compute_expected_max_sharpe, no Var(SR_k) term); dsr_gate.py:444-450 uses it directly as PSR threshold; l2_candidate_evidence_adapter.py:152-155 `observed_sharpe ← oos_sharpe` (per-candidate OOS trade series, per-period units); l2_ml_advisory_executor.py:1786-1800/_run_dsr_stage passes n_trials=n_eff with no trial_sharpes; n_eff_cluster.py MODULE_NOTE confirms n_eff (often >1) is the sole DSR n_trials feed. .claude/skills/walk-forward-validation-protocol/SKILL.md §2.2 writes SR_max_expected = sqrt(Var(SR))·(...). Mitigating context: alpha_wealth_refund_reconciler.py N-7 note ratifies 'discovery ≈ 0 初期是設計後果' via the separate wealth-derived threshold >=0.9995, and residual_alpha_gate defaults n_trials=1 (unaffected); when trial_sharpes are supplied, sample-max sidesteps the issue.
claims.impact: An un-ratified extra layer of over-deflation on the candidate discovery loop, compounding the ratified near-zero-pass wealth threshold; candidates with real deflated significance can never numerically pass the DSR stage when K>=2 and units are per-trade. Direction is conservative (no live-loss risk) but it is a structural evolution blocker for the profit-first loop and can silently invalidate any future decision to relax the wealth threshold.
claims.fix_hint: Scale E[max SR_k] by the cross-trial Sharpe standard deviation (estimated from trial_sharpes or a declared prior) per Bailey-LdP Eq.8, and assert/normalize the Sharpe unit contract (per-period SR with T=n_observations) at the compute_dsr boundary; add a unit test with realistic per-trade SR magnitudes.

claims.title: Global single third-vote reserve caps confirmed high-risk claims at one per full-audit run
claims.assertion: needsThird is true for every isHighRisk claim (CRITICAL severity or auth-bypass/secret-leak/missing-gate/leakage/replay-misuse defect types), but thirdAllowed holds only for the single global thirdVoteClaimId; quorum requires an eligible third whenever needsThird, so every high-risk claim except one is structurally forced to disputed even when both first-view verifiers unanimously confirm it, and is pushed to coverage debt ('global risk-conditioned third-vote reserve exhausted').
claims.evidence: .claude/workflows/openclaw-full-audit.js:927 `globalThirdVoteReserve = distinctClaims.length > 0 ? 1 : 0`; :964-968 thirdVoteClaimId selects exactly one claim; :1026-1027 `needsThird = isHighRisk(finding) || disagreement; thirdAllowed = needsThird && claim.claim_id === thirdVoteClaimId`; :1058 `quorum = firstComplete && (!needsThird || Boolean(eligibleThird))`; :1073 `disputed: !quorum`. Envelope math: 46 attempts − 13 audits − 1 seam − 2 retry reserve leaves ~30; 14 claims × 2 votes + 1 third = 29, so capacity, not value, sets the single slot.
claims.impact: An audit that surfaces multiple CRITICAL/auth-class claims can confirm at most one of them per run; the rest cannot reach quorum regardless of evidence and each requires a new cold-restart task (full re-discovery cost, since Full Audit continuation is unsupported at :610-612). The marginal cost of confirming the Nth CRITICAL claim is an entire audit run — a large rework annuity concentrated on precisely the highest-severity claims.
claims.fix_hint: Make the third-vote reserve risk-conditioned per admitted claim within remaining attempt/token capacity (trade later low-severity claim admissions for thirds on earlier CRITICAL ones), or admit a claims-only verification workflow that can consume immutable prior findings without re-running discovery.

claims.assertion: The committed run record claims evidence_class=PLATFORM_OR_EXTERNAL_ATTESTED for the trade-core target-host probe receipt, but the same document and the registry invariant state that the real out-of-band attestation (operator SSHSIG over the target-host execution bundle) is operator-gated and not yet performed; under the repo's own evidence-tier definitions (AGENTS.md: packet-local receipts cannot authenticate their own execution; the S1 signer public key in agent_governance_aiml_trusted_host.py is a placeholder that cannot validate anything), the receipt is at best ORCHESTRATOR_BOUND until that signature exists.
claims.evidence: docs/execution_plan/ai_ml_landing/receipts/S1-formal-closure-target-host-run-record.md:35 '| `evidence_class` | `PLATFORM_OR_EXTERNAL_ATTESTED` |' vs :11 'operator SSHSIG (operator-gated, §Pending)' and :85 terminal 'S1_ENGINEERING_CLOSED_EXTERNAL_WORM_BINDING_PENDING'. Registry invariant for target_host_disposable_runtime_probe_adapter_v1 (.codex/agent_registry_v1.json): 'real operator SSHSIG attestation is the out-of-band trusted-host step; no closure effect binding is real until the operator SSHSIG-signs the target-host execution bundle'. agent_governance_aiml_trusted_host.py: EXPECTED_S1_TARGET_HOST_SIGNER_FINGERPRINT='SHA256:OPERATOR-PROVIDES-…' placeholder.
claims.impact: A downstream closure consumer or later PM could read the committed receipt table as already externally attested and skip the SSHSIG binding, silently upgrading an unauthenticated self-recorded run to attested status. The honest BINDING_PENDING terminal and the fail-closed placeholder key materially mitigate; verdict integrity is intact, the label is what drifts.
claims.fix_hint: Relabel the committed projection as 'PLATFORM_OR_EXTERNAL_ATTESTED (claimed; pending operator SSHSIG binding — currently ORCHESTRATOR_BOUND from the closure's perspective)' or add an explicit assurance-status column, so the tier only reads as attested after the SSHSIG lands.

seam_reprobes.seam: CC's 'PLATFORM_OR_EXTERNAL_ATTESTED effect receipt with SSHSIG pending' + E2's 'target-host attested lane accepts non-replayable structural_reference_only capture' + OPS's 'Linux attestation OWED not discharged by pytest-only CI gate' are three independent partial findings about the same S1 effect-receipt evidence-tier claim, but no axis synthesizes them into one adjudicated verdict on whether the PLATFORM_OR_EXTERNAL_ATTESTED label itself is currently a false/overclaimed evidence tier (root-principle 8/12 exposure).
seam_reprobes.why: This is an authority-class question (evidence-tier truthfulness of a closure-relevant receipt), not a single-axis bug; CC must pull E2's non-replayability finding and OPS's CI-scope finding to render one combined verdict instead of leaving three separate partial signals that could each be individually dismissed as 'someone else's problem'.

seam_reprobes.seam: BB's D2 finding (fill-sim taker fee modeled at half Bybit's real 5.5bps/leg schedule) and QC's PROFIT-1 finding (cost_gate double-counts trading costs against a fee threshold) are both cost-model defects feeding the same promotion/edge-viability decision, but no one has computed whether they cancel, compound, or are orthogonal in net effect on shipped-candidate bps.
seam_reprobes.why: QC owns the promotion-gate/cost-accounting math and is best positioned to pull BB's fee-schedule evidence and quantify the combined directional bias; leaving them as two separately-filed findings risks the closure treating each as independently low-impact when together they could reverse or double a promotion decision.

seam_reprobes.seam: QC's own promotion-gate statistics findings (DSR Eq.8 scaling omission, CPCV not actually combinatorial, ad-hoc power formula with fixed effect size) and MIT's independently-derived 'ship-gate statistical power very low at n_test≈26' are not reconciled against each other — unclear whether MIT's finding is a downstream symptom of QC's same defective formulas (one root cause, redundant filing) or an independent confirming data point (two distinct roots, higher aggregate confidence).
seam_reprobes.why: QC is the quant-methodology authority for both gates; only QC can trace whether MIT's empirical power number derives from the same flawed DSR/power-formula code path QC flagged, which changes whether closure should record one root-cause defect or two independently corroborating ones.

seam_reprobes.seam: MIT's 'DSN split-string deliberately evades the public-repo credential scanner in ML persistence fallback' and OPS's 'restart_all.sh documents an in-code evasion of the same credential gate via split DSN literal' describe structurally identical anti-patterns in two different files/subsystems, but no axis checked whether this is one shared convention/helper (systemic, more instances likely) or two coincidentally similar one-offs, nor swept the repo for further occurrences.
seam_reprobes.why: This is a security/authority-boundary pattern (credential-gate bypass), which is CC's ownership; only a repo-wide grep for the split-string DSN pattern can settle whether remediation scope is 2 files or an unknown-N systemic convention, which materially changes severity and remediation ownership.

seam_reprobes.seam: Multiple axes (CC/FA/E2/OPS/MIT/E5/R4) independently rediscovered that TODO.md/PROGRESS.md/initiative_index.md are stale about S1 merge state, but none checked E5's separately-filed finding that 'the Registry active_state pack pins only the AI/ML dispatch board into every task capsule' as a possible root mechanism — i.e., whether the staleness is simply an un-edited document (content bug) or a symptom of the active_state pack-generation logic itself serving frozen/cached content into every task capsule regardless of source freshness.
seam_reprobes.why: E5 is the Registry/pack-generation owner and is the only axis positioned to check the pack's generation timestamp/refresh logic against source_head; if the pack mechanism itself is stale-by-design, seven axes independently re-filing the same symptom is masking one systemic Registry defect rather than a one-line doc edit.
